name: Krebs Cycle (Citric Acid Cycle)
description: Citric acid cycle: acetyl-CoA condenses with oxaloacetate and is oxidized through eight steps that release two CO2, three NADH, one FADH2 and one GTP per turn, regenerating oxaloacetate, with isocitrate dehydrogenase gated by the NADH/ATP level.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Acetyl-CoA enters"]
    N2["Citrate synthase: citrate"]
    N3["Aconitase: isocitrate"]
    N4{"NADH / ATP high?"}
    N5["Isocitrate DH: aKG + NADH"]
    N6["aKG DH: succinyl-CoA"]
    N7["Succinyl-CoA -> GTP"]
    N8["Succinate DH: FADH2"]
    N9["Fumarase: malate"]
    N10["Malate DH: OAA + NADH"]
    N11["OAA regenerated"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|proceed| N5
    N4 -->|NADH high: slow| N3
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 -->|next turn| N2

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The key role of anaplerosis and cataplerosis for citric acid cycle function
sources.authors: Owen, O. E.; Kalhan, S. C.; Hanson, R. W.

sources.title: The role of citric acid in intermediate metabolism in animal tissues
sources.authors: Krebs, H. A.; Johnson, W. A.
